title: Our secondary source cites the DECOY module R2BRON.MAC and misreads hex constants as decimal
claim: docs/red-baron-1980-source-findings.md §3 — the authority our three files cite in every header — sources every enemy fact to `R2BRON.MAC`, the build that did NOT ship. Worse, it states its own radix rule ("Constants in RBARON.MAC/RBGRND.MAC live in a .RADIX 16 region (RBARON.MAC:74) — hex unless written with a trailing dot") and then breaks it three times: ACCEL=30 (really 0x30 = 48), P.INDP=1080 (really 0x1080 = 4224), P.MNDP=140 (really 0x140 = 320) — while correctly reading P.OLIM/P.ILIM as hex in the very next sentence. The doc is internally inconsistent, and enemy.ts/returning-ace.ts copied the wrong three verbatim.
reasoning: RBARON.MAC:74 sets `.RADIX 16` and the only decimal island in the file is 6217-6280, so lines 464/465/469 are unambiguously hex. R2BRON.MAC differs from RBARON.MAC only in seven checksum bytes (CHKSM2..CHKSM7), so the enemy VALUES the doc quotes happen to be the same bytes — but the citation is invalid and the doc has already demonstrated it cannot be trusted to apply its own radix rule. Every downstream "[ROM-verified]" tag in §3 must be re-derived, not inherited. Fix: re-cite §3 to RBARON.MAC and correct the three constants.

ours.verbatim: `R2BRON.MAC:2566-2870`): the plane accelerates its ΔX (`ACCEL=30`) toward window limits and

title: P_INDP is 3.9x too shallow — 0x1080 = 4224, not 1080
claim: `P.INDP =1080` sits in the `.RADIX 16` region (RBARON.MAC:74), so the initial plane depth is 0x1080 = 4224, not decimal 1080. We spawn every plane at 1080 — 3.911x closer than the arcade (4224/1080 = 3.911). Both sides are in the same unit (ROM screen-window units, one value per calc frame), so no timebase conversion applies: the number is simply misread. STPLNE writes it to both the motion depth (PLSTAT+4/+5) and the display depth (PLSTAT+19/+1A).
reasoning: Enemies appear on top of the player from the first frame instead of as distant specks, which collapses the approach phase the whole game is paced around. Note the blast radius: scoring.ts:25 imports P_INDP to anchor its closeness scale (`leadScore`), so the fix must be made with scoring.ts in the same change or plane values will jump ~4x.

title: ACCEL is 0x30 = 48 per calc frame, not 30
claim: `ACCEL =30` is hex: 48 decimal. P.WCHK adds +ACCEL / -ACCEL to the 16-bit delta once per calc frame (RBARON.MAC:2844, :2846, :2851), and our step() likewise adds ACCEL once per 96 ms calc frame — SAME units, direct comparison, no 10.4167 conversion needed. We accelerate the weave at 30/frame where the arcade uses 48/frame: 30/48 = 0.625, i.e. our planes build turn rate at 62.5 % of arcade rate and therefore bank shallower (biplaneBank is proportional to deltaX).
reasoning: The doc that fed us this (findings §3) wrote `ACCEL=30` straight out of a hex region. RBARON.MAC:465 has no trailing dot, and the only .RADIX 10 island in the file is 6217-6280.

title: P_MNDP is 0x140 = 320, not 140 — the fly-by trigger fires 2.3x too late
claim: `P.MNDP =140` is hex: 320 decimal. UPDPLN tests the plane's motion depth against it (RBARON.MAC:2723-2726) and, when the plane is closer than 320, runs P.UPD0: it clears the plane, arms the returning attack, and sets the BEHIND YOU timer. We use 140 — the plane must get 2.286x closer (320/140) before the ace pass triggers. enemy.ts:63 MIN_DEPTH=140 carries the same error.
reasoning: Same radix miss as EN-002/EN-003, from the same doc line. Also worth noting: our proximityBand cutoffs (NEAR=300, MID=700) were chosen against a 1080-deep world; with P.INDP corrected to 4224 they are all inside the "very close" tenth of the approach and the DISCHK control-feel bands will effectively never leave `near`.

title: P.OLIM outer-window limits are correct — 0x40,0x80,0x120,0x1A0,0x200
claim: P.OLIM is five .WORDs at RBARON.MAC:2939-2943: 40, 80, 120, 1A0, 200 — in hex, 64, 128, 288, 416, 512, indexed by GMLEVL (word-indexed via SAVY = 2*min(GMLEVL,4)). Our P_OLIM = [0x40, 0x80, 0x120, 0x1a0, 0x200] matches byte-for-byte, and .LEVLS=5 (RBARON.MAC:504) matches our 5-entry table.
reasoning: Interesting that the SAME doc that fed us three decimal-misread constants got the two window tables right — evidence the tables were transcribed by a different pass than the scalars. Recording this as a match so the fix list stays honest.

ours.verbatim: export const P_OLIM: readonly number[] = Object.freeze([0x40, 0x80, 0x120, 0x1a0, 0x200])

title: P.ILIM inner-window limits are correct — 0x20,0x30,0x80,0x120,0x160
claim: P.ILIM is five .WORDs at RBARON.MAC:2945-2946: 20, 30, 80, 120, 160 — hex 32, 48, 128, 288, 352. Our P_ILIM = [0x20, 0x30, 0x80, 0x120, 0x160] matches exactly.
reasoning: The VALUES are right. What we do with them is not — see EN-013: P_ILIM is read only inside spawn() and plays no part in our weave, whereas in the ROM the inner window is the boundary that actually reverses the plane ("HEAD AWAY FROM CENTER", RBARON.MAC:2796).

ours.verbatim: export const P_ILIM: readonly number[] = Object.freeze([0x20, 0x30, 0x80, 0x120, 0x160])

title: The PLNLVL fire gate is exactly right — GMLEVL<4 never, ==4 coin flip, >=5 always
claim: NWPLNE:2349-2359 loads GMLEVL, defaults X=0xB0 (no D3), and: CMP I,4 / BCC 50$ -> GMLEVL < 4 keeps 0xB0 (NORMAL PLANE, never shoots); CMP I,5 / BEQ 45$ -> GMLEVL == 5 takes 0xB8 (@ PLAYER); otherwise JSR RANDOM / LSR / BCS 50$ -> a 50 % coin flip on the RANDOM LSB. planeFireChance(level) returns 0 for lvl<4, 0.5 for lvl==4, 1 otherwise — the same three-way gate, same thresholds.
reasoning: The 0xB8 - 0xB0 = 8 difference is status bit D3, which PLNSHL:4805 (`AND I,8`) tests before firing — matching the "@ SHOOTS" bit in the PLOBDB glossary. Confirmed both ends. (The only wrinkle, out of our three files: a drone PROMOTED to lead by UPPLX1 gets 0xA8 only when GMLEVL >= 5 — RBARON.MAC:3023-3028 — with no level-4 coin flip. We have no promotion-time fire gate at all, but promotion lives in waves.ts.)

title: Plane fire cadence is the calc-frame LSB, even frames — and the phase is NOT inferred, the ROM pins it
claim: PLNSHL: `LDA FRAME / LSR / BCS 10$ ;EVERY OTHER FRAME` — LSR shifts FRAME bit 0 into carry, and BCS skips the launch. So the plane fires exactly when FRAME is EVEN: 1 shot per 2 calc frames = 10.4167 / 2 = 5.208 shots/s. FRAME is incremented once per MAIN iteration (RBARON.MAC:870), i.e. once per 96 ms calculation frame — the same clock our simFrame counts. planeFires() gates on `(frame & 1) !== 0 -> return false`, firing on even frames. Exact match, including the parity.
reasoning: Our comment at enemy.ts:200-201 says "which frame parity fires is inferred (the ROM pins the ÷2, not the phase)". That is wrong — RBARON.MAC:4809-4811 pins the phase too, and we happened to guess it right. Worth deleting the hedge so nobody "fixes" it to odd.

ours.verbatim: if ((Math.floor(frame) & 1) !== 0) return false // ÷2 FRAME cadence — hold fire on odd frames

title: HARD_TURN = 0x1C is the exact break-turn threshold
claim: EOLSEQ takes |PLDELX| via SPABS and compares `CMP I,1C` (hex 28); BCC -> "PLAYER DIES". So the player must hold |PLDELX| >= 0x1C = 28 to have any chance of shaking the ace. returning-ace.ts HARD_TURN = 0x1c, used as `Math.abs(turnRate) >= HARD_TURN`. Same value, same inclusive >=.
reasoning: The threshold is right. The logic around it is not — see EN-011 and EN-012.

title: The 25 % lone-plane roll is real — RANDOM AND 0x30 is a 2-bit test
claim: STPLNE:2345-2348: `JSR RANDOM / AND I,30 / BNE 40$ / STA N.OBJ ;RANDOM (0.25) ONLY 1 PLANE`. Mask 0x30 is two bits; both are zero with probability 1/4, and only then is N.OBJ (the drone bitmask) zeroed. Our LONE_PLANE_CHANCE = 0.25 is the same probability, and the developer's own comment says so.
reasoning: Confirming the value. Note that in the ROM the roll ZEROES an already-computed count (so it can only ever REDUCE a 2- or 3-plane wave to 1), which is exactly how waves.ts:77-79 uses it.

title: Clamping GMLEVL to 4 for the window tables is correct — GMLEVL really can be 5
claim: P.WINDW:2756-2759 does `LDA GMLEVL / CMP I,5 / BCC 5$ / LDA I,4` — GMLEVL 5 is clamped to table index 4 before the *2 word-index. GMLEVL genuinely reaches 5: PLNLVL (RBARON.MAC:2478) is `.BYTE 0,0,0,0,1,2,2,2,3,3,3,4,4,4,4,4,5`, indexed by min(OBJKLD>>1, 16), so 32+ kills gives GMLEVL=5 — SIX levels, even though .LEVLS=5 sizes the tables. enemy.ts levelIndex() clamps to P_OLIM.length-1 = 4, doing exactly the same thing, while planeFireChance() correctly lets lvl>=5 through as always-fire.
reasoning: This is the one place where "5 game levels" and a 6-valued GMLEVL could have bitten us, and we get it right on both paths by accident of structure. Recording it.

ours.verbatim: const levelIndex = (level: number): number => clamp(Math.floor(level) || 0, 0, P_OLIM.length - 1)

title: The ace evade check has the CORRECT-SIDE polarity INVERTED — we reward banking the wrong way
claim: EOLSEQ:1081-1083: `LDA ENSIDE / EOR PLDELX / BPL 20$ ;SORRY WRONG X`. ENSIDE holds D7 of the plane's screen X (RBARON.MAC:2735) — 0x80 when X is negative. EOR of the two sign bits: BPL (result D7 = 0) means the signs were the SAME, and 20$ is the death path. So the player survives only when sign(PLDELX) is OPPOSITE to sign(plane X) — you break AWAY from the side he came from. returning-ace.ts:121 requires `Math.sign(turnRate) === ace.side` — the SAME sign. We have the escape direction exactly backwards: a player who does the arcade-correct thing dies, and a player who does the arcade-fatal thing is rewarded.
reasoning: This is a one-character fix (`===` -> `!==`, or negate `ace.side` at beginPass) but it inverts the meaning of the game's single most famous mechanic. Our module header at returning-ace.ts:27 even flags the polarity as INFERRED ("turn TOWARD the shoulder he came from") — the source settles it, and the inference was wrong.

ours.verbatim: const skillDodge = Math.sign(turnRate) === ace.side && Math.abs(turnRate) >= HARD_TURN

title: The ace evade BRANCH ORDER is inverted — the freebie is a REWARD for a good break-turn, not a bypass of it
claim: In the ROM the skill test is the GATE and the BEFLAG freebie / 50-50 sits BEHIND it. EOLSEQ, in order: wrong side -> 20$ (die); |PLDELX| < 0x1C -> `BCC 20$ ;PLAYER DIES` (die); ONLY having passed both, `BIT BEFLAG / BPL 17$ ;FIRST TIME FREE` -> survive and consume the flag; on any later pass, `JSR RANDOM / BMI 20$ ;AFTER - ONLY 50/50`. So: turn wrong or turn softly and you die OUTRIGHT — no freebie, no coin flip. Ours is the mirror image (returning-ace.ts:118-125): firstPass -> evaded unconditionally (even standing still); else skill dodge -> evaded; else -> a free 50/50 for a player who did nothing. The ROM never grants a coin flip to a player who failed the break-turn; we never punish one.
reasoning: Net effect on difficulty: the arcade ace is a skill check with a mercy roll; ours is a mercy roll with an optional skill check. Combined with EN-012 (inverted polarity), a player cannot learn the mechanic from our game at all — which is a strong candidate for "seems way off". Not a constant tweak: evadeCheck's three-branch nesting has to be restructured, and the "first attack is free" semantics change from unconditional to conditional-on-a-good-turn.

title: PLPOSZ is wrong in every respect — sign, magnitude, length, and the level ramp (20x vs our 2.5x)
claim: PLPOSZ (RBARON.MAC:2482) is `.BYTE -4,-10,-20,-30,-40,-50,-60,-70,-80` in a hex region: -4, -16, -32, -48, -64, -80, -96, -112, -128 — NINE entries, NEGATIVE (added to the display depth at RBARON.MAC:2704-2707, so depth falls), indexed by GMLEVL 0..5 (PLNZD:2409-2411), i.e. only -4..-80 are ever reached. The arcade ramp is therefore 4/frame at GMLEVL 0 to 80/frame at GMLEVL 5 — a 20x acceleration in closing rate across the game. Ours is [8, 10, 13, 16, 20]: five entries, positive, ramping 8 -> 20 = 2.5x, and level 0 is 2x too fast while level 5 is 4x too slow.
reasoning: And it is worse than the numbers suggest: NOTHING imports closeSpeed() or PLPOSZ — a grep across src/ shows returning-ace.ts is imported by no module. enemy.ts:161 uses its own invented CLOSE_SPEED = 8, flat at every level, so the difficulty ramp the ROM builds out of PLPOSZ does not exist in our game at all. That is very likely the core of "difficulty pacing seems way off": the arcade gets 20x harder to line up a shot on; we get 0x harder.

ours.verbatim: export const PLPOSZ: readonly number[] = Object.freeze([8, 10, 13, 16, 20])

title: THE BIG ONE: the weave is a THREE-ZONE, TABLE-DRIVEN, TWO-AXIS velocity servo — our hand-written stepper cannot express it at any constant setting
claim:
P.WINDW/P.WCHK (RBARON.MAC:2755-2874) is not "accelerate toward a cap and reverse at the wall". It is: (1) classify the plane's |position| into one of THREE zones against the level-indexed windows — outside P.OLIM, between P.OLIM and P.ILIM, inside P.ILIM; (2) select a TARGET |delta| from one of three parallel level-indexed tables laid out contiguously and addressed by a computed Y = zone_base + 2*min(GMLEVL,4) (RBARON.MAC:2791 `.LEVLS*2`, :2797 `.LEVLS*4`, :2806 `LDA AY,P.ODLX`): P.ODLX = 2x{90,8C,84,7C},80*2 = 288,280,264,248,256 (outer); P.IDLX = 3x{8,14,1C,24},2C*3 = 24,60,84,108,132 (middle); P.IIDL = 3x{0,10,18,28},40*3 = 0,48,72,120,192 (inner). (3) Derive the sign from FLAG+1 = sign(position) EOR sign(delta), INVERTED inside the inner window ("REVERSE FLAG (HEAD AWAY FROM CENTER)", :2796), FORCED to no-change in the middle band ("NO DIRECTION CHANGES", :2789-2790), and inverted outside the outer window ONLY when the display depth PLSTAT+19 < 4 (:2776-2781). (4) Servo the current delta toward that target by +/-ACCEL, SNAPPING to the target when within one ACCEL step (:2830-2840). (5) Then `P.WITR: DEX / DEX / ... / JMP P.WINDW` — run the ENTIRE machine a SECOND time on the OTHER AXIS. PLNDEL enters with X=2 (the Y axis, biased by HORIZN=0x40) and the loop re-enters with X=0 (the X axis). The planes weave in TWO dimensions.

Ours (enemy.ts:148-163) is: one axis; one invented WEAVE_SPEED_CAP = 100; reverse at the OUTER boundary; no zones; no target tables; y frozen at its spawn value forever.
reasoning: There is no assignment of constants to WEAVE_SPEED_CAP / ACCEL / P_OLIM that reproduces this. The ROM has three DIFFERENT target speeds per level selected by a zone the plane is currently in, and the reversal happens at the INNER window, not the outer — so the arcade plane sweeps out to the outer limit, coasts, then is turned back only when it penetrates the inner window near screen centre. Ours reverses at the outer wall and never consults P_ILIM at all outside spawn(). And the whole thing runs twice per plane, on Y as well as X. Answering the structural question put to me directly: this is a REWRITE, not a tuning pass. It is not a bytecode VM (as in the sibling game) — it is a per-axis three-zone table-driven servo — but the conclusion is the same: our hand-written stepper is the wrong shape and must be replaced with the zone/target-table structure. Size l.

ours.verbatim: const deltaX = clamp(enemy.deltaX + ACCEL * heading, -WEAVE_SPEED_CAP, WEAVE_SPEED_CAP)

title: We reverse the weave at the OUTER window; the ROM reverses it at the INNER window
claim: P.INER (RBARON.MAC:2794-2797) is reached only when |position| < P.ILIM, and it does `LDA FLAG+1 / EOR I,0FF / STA FLAG+1 ;REVERSE FLAG (HEAD AWAY FROM CENTER)`. That is the reversal: the plane is turned around when it gets CLOSE TO SCREEN CENTRE, and it then accelerates toward the P.IIDL target (0,48,72,120,192 by level). Outside the outer window the direction is inverted only conditionally, and only to bring an off-screen plane back (RBARON.MAC:2776-2781). enemy.ts:152-154 does the opposite: `if (enemy.x >= olim) heading = -1; else if (enemy.x <= -olim) heading = 1; else heading = deltaX >= 0 ? 1 : -1` — reverse at the OUTER wall, never at the inner. P_ILIM is imported at enemy.ts:38 and read exactly once, in spawn().
reasoning: Consequence: our plane bounces wall-to-wall across the full +/-P_OLIM window at a constant cap, a metronome. The arcade plane threads the inner window, gets kicked away from centre, decelerates in the middle band, and only reverses again on the next centre approach — a genuinely irregular weave whose amplitude and period are BOTH level-dependent (via P.IIDL/P.IDLX). Note also P.IIDL[0] = 0: at GMLEVL 0 the "head away from centre" target speed is ZERO, so the level-0 plane essentially parks near centre — the gentlest possible opener, which we do not reproduce. Subsumed by the EN-015 rewrite; listed separately because it is the single behavioural difference a player would name first.

source.verbatim: STA FLAG+1		;REVERSE FLAG (HEAD AWAY FROM CENTER)

title: Enemy planes never move VERTICALLY — the ROM runs the same weave machine on the Y axis
claim: PLNDEL enters the window machine with `LDX I,2 ;CENTER CROSSED` and loads PLSTAT+8+X = PLSTAT+0A — the Y screen position — biased by HORIZN (0x40 = 64): RBARON.MAC:2747-2754. P.WITR then does DEX/DEX to X=0 and `JMP P.WINDW` (RBARON.MAC:2865-2873), re-running the whole zone/target/servo machine on the X axis. UPDPLN:2582-2611 integrates the Y delta (PLSTAT+0E/+0F) into the Y position every frame and clamps it to PFPLOW = 0x80*4 = 0x200 = 512 and PFPHI = 0x140*4 = 0x500 = 1280. Our Enemy.y is set once in spawn() (enemy.ts:128, a +/-40 random) and step() carries it through untouched — our planes fly on a perfectly flat plane forever.
reasoning: Half the arcade's evasion is vertical. A plane that only slides left-right is a fairground duck. Part of the EN-015 rewrite.

title: The plane never flies PAST you — we floor the depth at MIN_DEPTH and it hovers there forever
claim: When a plane's motion depth (PLSTAT+4/+5) crosses below P.MNDP, UPDPLN falls into P.UPD0 and the plane is DESTROYED as an object: `STA PLSTAT+6 ;CLR PLANE` (RBARON.MAC:2741), with PLSTAT+7 = WO.RTN (0x10 = 16 calc frames = 1.54 s) as a re-entry delay, PLVALU zeroed, GMEND0 = 0xC0 arming the returning attack, and MESTM2 = 0x10 setting the BEHIND YOU timer. The plane has flown past your ear and is coming back. enemy.ts:161 instead does `depth: Math.max(enemy.depth - CLOSE_SPEED, MIN_DEPTH)` — the plane parks at depth 140 and weaves in your face indefinitely; nothing in our sim ever retires it or hands it to returning-ace.ts.
reasoning: This is why the returning-ace module is dead code (EN-019): enemy.ts never produces the event that would drive it. The fix is coupled to EN-015 — depth must be an unclamped integrator with a "past you" terminal state, not a clamped decrement.

title: The entire returning-ace mechanic is DEAD CODE — nothing in src/ imports returning-ace.ts
claim: In the ROM the ace pass is wired into the main loop unconditionally: MAIN calls `JSR EOLSEQ ;END OF LIFE SEQUENCE` every calc frame (RBARON.MAC:825), EOLSEQ sprays enemy shells from behind while the returning plane is inbound (RBARON.MAC:1077), and runs the evade check on the exact frame PLSTAT+7 counts down to 0x0C (RBARON.MAC:1078-1079) — i.e. 4 calc frames (384 ms) after the fly-by. In our tree, `grep -rn "closesPast|evadeCheck|beginPass|closeSpeed|P_MNDP" src/` returns hits ONLY inside returning-ace.ts itself. main.ts imports enemy.ts, waves.ts, blimp.ts, guns.ts, flight.ts — never returning-ace.ts. The signature "BEHIND YOU / bank hard to shake him" mechanic — the thing Red Baron is remembered for — is implemented, unit-tested, and never executed.
reasoning: Every constant in returning-ace.ts is therefore moot until it is wired up, which is why I have flagged EN-012/EN-013/EN-014 as fix-worthy anyway: they will be wrong the moment someone connects it. Sizing m because the wiring depends on EN-018 (enemy.ts must first be able to emit a fly-by event).

title: Drones have their own two-phase behaviour (PARALLEL -> FREE); ours weave exactly like leads
claim: A drone is created with status byte = 2 ("FORMATION FLIGHT (=2)", RBARON.MAC:2368/:2382) and X/Y stored as OFFSETS from the lead: PLANE1 = .WORD -100,100 and PLANE2 = .WORD -100,-100 (RBARON.MAC:2480-2481) — hex, so (-256, +256) and (-256, -256). Parallel drones do not steer at all; they are rigidly pinned to the lead. When the lead rotates away, UPDMOB calls FREPAR (RBARON.MAC:2653), and FRDRNE tests `CMP I,2 ;PARALLEL` then `LSR ZX,PLOBDB+6 ;FREE DRONE` (2 >> 1 = 1) and converts the offsets to ABSOLUTE positions (RBARON.MAC:3511-3528). From then on the drone runs UPDRNE, which is a completely different stepper: it creeps at a FIXED +/-0x10 = 16 units per calc frame toward a hard-coded destination — DRPOST = .WORD 160,300,-180,380 (RBARON.MAC:3135), hex, so drone 0 heads for (352, 768) and drone 1 for (-384, 896) — with a screen clamp at 0x05C0 and an optional "growth" phase (Z decremented 0x60 = 96/frame down to 0x0E00) for the DRNSET-selected drone. Our enemy.ts has an EnemyKind discriminant but step() (enemy.ts:148) ignores it entirely, and main.ts:395 maps EVERY enemy — leads and drones alike — through the same weave.
reasoning: No constant in enemy.ts expresses "rigid formation offset from a parent" or "creep toward a fixed destination", so there is nothing to compare against; this is genuinely absent. Three distinct steppers are needed (lead weave / parallel drone / free drone) where we have one. Part of the EN-015 rewrite; sized l on its own because the parallel->free promotion needs a lead-relative coordinate mode that our flat Enemy record cannot hold.

title: The 90-degree entry bank is discarded on the very first step()
claim: STPLNE sets PLSTAT+15 = 0x02 or 0xFE — `SET ROTATION (X/Z) 90 DEGREES` (RBARON.MAC:2307) — and status D5/D4, and gives the plane PLSTAT+7 = 0x20 = 32 calc frames (3.07 s) "TO CROSS CENTER" (RBARON.MAC:2361). While D5 is set, PLNDEL returns immediately (RBARON.MAC:2743-2746) — the plane does NOT weave — and UPDMOB rolls the rotation back toward level at +/-0x40 = 64 units per calc frame (RBARON.MAC:2638/:2641) until it reaches zero, at which point FORMTN clears D5 and the weave begins. The entry is a multi-second banked arc. Our spawn() sets `bank: side * SPAWN_BANK` (pi/2) and `deltaX: 0` (enemy.ts:134-135), but step() unconditionally overwrites bank with `biplaneBank(deltaX)` on the FIRST frame — so our 90-degree entry bank exists for exactly zero frames of motion and is never seen.
reasoning: Also: the arcade plane enters with a real entry velocity, MAXDEL = -0x180 = -384 (RBARON.MAC:470, written to both the X and Y deltas at RBARON.MAC:2298-2309), not the 0 we spawn with. Two separate misses — no rollout state machine, and no entry velocity.

title: The blimp's 25 % spawn chance is right (RANDOM AND 0x0C is a 2-bit test)
claim: NWPLNE:2328-2331: `JSR RANDOM / AND I,0C / BNE 25$ / JSR CINTBP ;RANDOM BLIMP`. Mask 0x0C is two bits; both zero with probability 1/4. Our BLIMP_SPAWN_CHANCE = 0.25 is the same probability.
reasoning: The probability is right; the gate in front of it is not — see EN-023.

title: The blimp can appear from the very first wave — the ROM locks it behind four planes
claim: The 25 % roll is guarded: `LDA N.PLNZ / CMP I,4 / BCC 25$` (RBARON.MAC:2325-2327) — N.PLNZ counts every plane that has appeared this game (incremented at RBARON.MAC:2398), and the blimp cannot spawn until FOUR planes have been fielded. Our shouldSpawnBlimp(roll) (blimp.ts:94-95) is the bare 25 % roll with no counter gate at all, so a blimp can be the first thing a player ever sees. The ROM also picks the entry side deterministically from the player's own turn (`BIT PLDELX / JSR DPABS ;-X SIDE`, RBARON.MAC:1429-1430) at |X| = 0x03C0 = 960; our spawn() (blimp.ts:107-108) picks the side from the Rng at |X| in [180, 300).
reasoning: Two separate divergences on the same call: a missing unlock gate and a wrong entry geometry. Both are small; the unlock gate needs a plane counter threaded from main.ts.

title: The blimp fires 2x too fast and has no level gate — ROM is 1-in-4 frames AND requires GMLEVL >= 2
claim: The blimp does not use PLNSHL. BLMOTN calls `JSR SHLAUN ;LAUNCH SHELL @ PLAYER` (RBARON.MAC:4229), and SHLAUN opens `LDA FRAME / AND I,3 ;1 OUT OF 4 FRAMES / BEQ SHLAU0` (RBARON.MAC:4027-4030) — one shot per FOUR calc frames = 10.4167 / 4 = 2.604 shots/s. SHLAU0 then hard-gates on level: `LDX GMLEVL / DEX / DEX / BMI SHLAUX ;NO GROUND SHELLS @ LOWER LEVELS` (RBARON.MAC:4038-4041) — GMLEVL < 2 fires NOTHING. Our blimpFires(frame) (blimp.ts:143-145) is `(frame & 1) === 0` — one shot per TWO frames = 5.208 shots/s, exactly 2x the arcade rate — with no level gate whatsoever.
reasoning: The module header at blimp.ts:20-23 explicitly claims the opposite as a design fact: "unlike a plane, whose @ PLAYER bit is PLNLVL level-gated, the blimp is a threat whenever it is present... a menace even in the early sky the planes leave quiet." The source says the reverse: at GMLEVL 0-1 the blimp is completely harmless, and it cannot even spawn before four planes have flown. Our blimp is roughly the most dangerous thing in the early game; in the arcade it is the least. Doubly wrong, and it lands precisely in the early-game window a new player judges the game by.

ours.verbatim: return (Math.floor(frame) & 1) === 0 // ÷2 FRAME cadence — fire on even frames

title: The blimp is an APPROACHING airship on a 2.9-second fuse, not a constant-depth lateral drifter
claim: INITBP puts the blimp at Z = 0x1000 = 4096 (BLOBJ+5 = 0x10, +4 = 0; RBARON.MAC:1425-1426) and gives it an X velocity of 0xFFF8 = -8 units/frame, sign-flipped by the entry side (RBARON.MAC:1443-1448). BLMOTN then decrements Z by a 16-bit -0x0080 = -128 EVERY calc frame (`ADC I,-80` / `ADC I,-1`, RBARON.MAC:4260-4265) and clears the object when the Z MSB drops below 1, i.e. Z < 0x100 = 256 (RBARON.MAC:4266-4270). So the blimp's whole life is (4096 - 256) / 128 = 30 calc frames = 30 x 96 ms = 2.88 seconds, during which it BEARS DOWN on you at 128/frame while sliding sideways at only 8/frame — a 16:1 closing-to-drift ratio. Ours (blimp.ts:54, :57, :130) cruises at a CONSTANT depth of 600, drifts at 12/frame, never closes, and never expires — a 0:12 closing-to-drift ratio, i.e. the exact inverse of the arcade motion.
reasoning: blimp.ts:12-17 states as design intent that "the blimp does the opposite [of the plane]: ONE steady drift... and cruises at a constant depth; the motion is purely lateral." That is a fabrication — the ROM blimp is almost purely a closer. The module header is candid that BLMOTN was "not byte-transcribed in the quarry" and that the depth/drift were invented; it is byte-transcribable, and the invention is backwards. Player-visible effect: our blimp floats past as scenery, the arcade blimp is a timed threat you must destroy before it reaches you.

title: The blimp YAWS as it approaches; our Blimp has no heading at all
claim: INITBP sets a Y-axis (heading) angle of +/-0x0200 at BLOBJ+14/+15 (`STA BLOBJ+15 ;Y ANGLE MSB`, RBARON.MAC:1439-1442), and BLMOTN rotates it toward zero by +/-0x28 = 40 units per calc frame (RBARON.MAC:4281-4297), while YAXIS (RBARON.MAC:4211-4214) applies it to the geometry — the airship swings to face you as it closes. Our Blimp record has `bank` (roll, pinned to 0) and no heading field of any kind, so there is nothing to compare; the rotation simply does not exist in our model.
reasoning: Our `bank: 0` is not itself wrong — the ROM never rolls the blimp either. What is missing is the ORTHOGONAL axis: a yaw that starts at 0x200 and unwinds at 40/frame over the blimp's ~30-frame life (0x200 / 40 = 12.8 frames to level out). Classed NO_COUNTERPART rather than DIVERGENCE because there is no field of ours to cite. Cosmetic relative to EN-024/EN-025; accept unless the blimp is being reworked anyway, in which case it is trivial to add alongside.
